name: get_battery
description: Get the phone's current battery level (percent), charging state, temperature and voltage.

name: set_flashlight
description: Turn the phone flashlight (torch) on or off, or toggle it.

state.description: One of On, Off, or Toggle

name: send_morse
description: Blink a short text message in Morse code on the phone's flashlight. Use for fun, signalling, or device-to-device light chat. Keep the text short (a few words) — Morse is slow.

text.description: The text to flash in Morse (letters, digits, basic punctuation).

name: get_memory
description: Get the device RAM usage and total capacity.

name: set_volume
description: Set a system audio volume stream to a level.

name: notify
description: Show a system notification (heads-up) to the user with a title and body text.

name: vibrate
description: Vibrate the phone for a short duration in milliseconds.

name: get_network
description: Get the phone's network status — Wi-Fi/cellular, SSID, IP.

name: list_apps
description: List installed apps on the phone.

name: rd_consult
description: Ask a more powerful online AI a question you can't answer on your own, and get its answer back. Use only when the user wants up-to-date or deep info beyond your local knowledge.

question.description: The question to ask the online AI

name: offer_choices
description: Offer the user a few tappable follow-up choices, shown as chips above the input. Use after answering when there are natural next things the user might want to do or ask. Give 2–5 short choices, each phrased as the message that will be sent if tapped.

choices.description: 2–5 short tappable suggestions, each the literal message to send when tapped.

name: make_card
description: Create a live widget card on the user's desktop (the Surface). A card is an Adaptive Card JSON template plus an optional PowerShell command whose JSON result fills the template's ${property} bindings. Use simple ${name} bindings that match the command's output properties. Set place=true to put it on the desktop immediately.

id.description: Card id: lowercase letters/digits/dashes, 2-32 chars, e.g. 'uptime'

template.description: The Adaptive Card JSON as a STRING: {"type":"AdaptiveCard","version":"1.5","body":[...]} with ${property} bindings

command.description: PowerShell that outputs an object whose properties fill the template bindings (optional for static cards)

place.description: true = also place it on the desktop now

name: remove_card
description: Remove a card you previously made from the desktop and the registry, by its id.

name: run_powershell
description: Run a PowerShell command in the on-device Subsystem runspace and get its JSON result. Use this for anything not covered by a dedicated tool — listing processes, packages, files, network, launching apps, or any Android cmdlet (Get-AndroidNetwork, Get-InstalledApp, Start-AndroidIntent, etc.).
